name: servicex

metadata.name: servicex
metadata.summary: Python SDK and CLI Client for ServiceX

documentation:
# ServiceX Client Library


[![GitHub Actions Status](https://github.com/ssl-hep/ServiceX_frontend/workflows/CI/CD/badge.svg?branch=master)](https://github.com/ssl-hep/ServiceX_frontend/actions)
[![Code Coverage](https://codecov.io/gh/ssl-hep/ServiceX_frontend/graph/badge.svg)](https://codecov.io/gh/ssl-hep/ServiceX_frontend)

[![PyPI version](https://badge.fury.io/py/servicex.svg)](https://badge.fury.io/py/servicex)
[![conda-forge version](https://img.shields.io/conda/vn/conda-forge/servicex.svg)](https://github.com/conda-forge/servicex-feedstock.git)
[![Supported Python versions](https://img.shields.io/pypi/pyversions/servicex.svg)](https://pypi.org/project/servicex/)

[![Docs](https://readthedocs.org/projects/docs/badge/?version=latest)](https://servicex-frontend.readthedocs.io)


Python SDK and CLI Client for ServiceX

## Documentation

* The **user** documentation is hosted at https://servicex-frontend.readthedocs.io
* The **developer** documentation is available under the [`latest` view](https://servicex-frontend.readthedocs.io/en/latest/)

methods.name: servicex.configuration.Configuration.expand_cache_path
documentation:
Expand the cache path to a full path, and create it if it doesn't exist.
Expand \$\{USER} to be the user name on the system. Works for windows, too.
\:param v:
\:return:

methods.name: servicex.configuration.Configuration.read
documentation:
Read configuration from .servicex or servicex.yaml file.
\:param config_path: If provided, use this as the path to the .servicex file.

> Otherwise, search, starting from the current working directory
> and look in enclosing directories

```
return

Populated configuration object
```

Returns True if request is submitted
Returns False if the request is not in the cache at all
or not submitted

methods.name: servicex.expandable_progress.ExpandableProgress.__init__
documentation:
We want to be able to use rich progress bars in the async code, but there are
some situtations where the user doesn't want them. Also we might be running
several simultaneous progress bars, and we want to be able to control that.

We still want to keep the context manager interface, so this class implements
the context manager but if display_progress is False, then it does nothing.
If provided_progress is set then we just use that. Otherwise we create a new
progress bar

```
param display_progress



param provided_progress

```

methods.name: servicex.expandable_progress.ExpandableProgress.__enter__
documentation:
Start the progress bar if it is not already started and the user wants one.
:return:

methods.name: servicex.expandable_progress.ExpandableProgress.__exit__
documentation:
Close the progress bar if it is not already closed and the user wanted one in
the first place.
\:param exc_type:
\:param exc_val:
\:param exc_tb:
\:return:

functions.name: servicex.func_adl.util.has_tuple
documentation:
Determine if this query used tuples in its final result

NOTE: This can't do depth searches in a really complex
query - then you need to follow best practices.

Args:
    a (ast.AST): The query

Returns:
    bool: True if the final Select statement has tuples or not.

functions.name: servicex.func_adl.util.has_col_names
documentation:
Determine if any column names were specified
in this request.

Args:
    a (ast.AST): The complete AST of the request.

Returns:
    bool: True if no column names were specified, False otherwise.

classes.name: servicex.dataset_identifier.DataSetIdentifier
documentation:
Base class for specifying the dataset to transform. This can either be a list of
xRootD URIs or a rucio DID

methods.name: servicex.dataset_identifier.RucioDatasetIdentifier.__init__
documentation:
Rucio Dataset - this will be looked up using the Rucio data management
service.

```
param dataset

The rucio DID - this can be a dataset or a container of datasets.

param num_files

Maximum number of files to return. This is useful during development
to perform quick runs. ServiceX is careful to make sure it always
returns the same subset of files.
```

methods.name: servicex.dataset_identifier.FileListDataset.__init__
documentation:
Dataset specified as a list of XRootD URIs.

```
param files

Either a list of URIs or a single URI string
```

methods.name: servicex.dataset_identifier.CERNOpenDataDatasetIdentifier.__init__
documentation:
CERN Open Data Dataset - this will be looked up using the CERN Open Data DID finder.

```
param dataset

The dataset ID - this is an integer.

param num_files

Maximum number of files to return. This is useful during development
to perform quick runs. ServiceX is careful to make sure it always
returns the same subset of files.
```

methods.name: servicex.dataset_identifier.XRootDDatasetIdentifier.__init__
documentation:
CERN Open Data Dataset - this will be looked up using the CERN Open Data DID finder.

```
param dataset

The dataset ID - this is an integer.

param num_files

Maximum number of files to return. This is useful during development
to perform quick runs. ServiceX is careful to make sure it always
returns the same subset of files.
```

classes.name: servicex.uproot_raw.uproot_raw.TreeSubQuery

class_variables.name: servicex.uproot_raw.uproot_raw.TreeSubQuery.treename
documentation: Name of input ntuple in file

class_variables.name: servicex.uproot_raw.uproot_raw.TreeSubQuery.expressions
documentation: Awkward Array expressions to evaluate for output

class_variables.name: servicex.uproot_raw.uproot_raw.TreeSubQuery.cut
documentation: Awkward Array expressions to select passing rows

class_variables.name: servicex.uproot_raw.uproot_raw.TreeSubQuery.filter_name
documentation: Expression to filter the list of considered input variables by name

class_variables.name: servicex.uproot_raw.uproot_raw.TreeSubQuery.filter_typename
documentation: Expression to filter the list of considered input variables by type name

class_variables.name: servicex.uproot_raw.uproot_raw.TreeSubQuery.aliases
documentation: Define aliases to use in computation and expressions

Express an uproot-raw query on an ntuple. Arguments have the same meaning as the
ones of the same name for uproot.arrays():
https://uproot.readthedocs.io/en/stable/uproot.behaviors.TBranch.HasBranches.html#uproot-behaviors-tbranch-hasbranches-arrays

classes.name: servicex.uproot_raw.uproot_raw.CopyHistogramSubQuery

class_variables.name: servicex.uproot_raw.uproot_raw.CopyHistogramSubQuery.copy_histograms
documentation: Objects to copy
Request the copying of a ROOT object from the input file to the output.

classes.name: servicex.query_core.ServiceXException
documentation: Something happened while trying to carry out a ServiceX request

methods.name: servicex.query_core.Query.__init__
documentation:
This is the main class for constructing transform requests and receiving the
results in a format of your choice. It can be run synchronously or asynchronously.

```
param dataset_identifier

Either a Rucio DID or a list of files

param title

Human readable title for this transform

param codegen

Name of the code generator

param sx_adapter



param config



param query_cache



param servicex_polling_interval

How many seconds between polling for
transform status?

param minio_polling_interval

How many seconds between polling the minio bucket
for new files?

param result_format



param ignore_cache

If true, ignore the cache and always submit a new transform

param fail_if_incomplete

If true, raise an exception if we don't have 100% completion
```

methods.name: servicex.query_core.Query.set_result_format
documentation:
Set the result format - required at constructor time or as part of the query
chain of methods
\:param result_format:
\:return: self to allow you to chain together query and setup methods

methods.name: servicex.query_core.Query.submit_and_download
documentation:
Submit the transform request to ServiceX. Poll the transform status to see when
the transform completes and to get the number of files in the dataset along with
current progress and failed file count.

```
param signed_urls_only

Set to true to skip actually downloading the files and
just return pre-signed urls

param display_progress

Set to false to disable the progress bar

param expandable_progress

Provide an existing progress bar. Set to None to have
one created for you

return

Transform results object which contains the list of files downloaded
or the list of pre-signed urls
```

methods.name: servicex.query_core.Query.transform_status_listener
documentation:
Poll ServiceX for the status of a transform. Update progress bars and keep track
of status. Once we know the number of files in the dataset, update the progress
bars.

methods.name: servicex.query_core.Query.download_files
documentation:
Task to monitor the list of files in the transform output's bucket. Any new files
will be downloaded.

methods.name: servicex.query_core.Query.as_files_async
documentation:
Submit the transform and request all the resulting files to be downloaded
\:return: TransformResult instance with the list of complete paths to the downloaded files

methods.name: servicex.query_core.Query.as_signed_urls_async
documentation:
Presign URLs for each of the transformed files

```
return

TransformedResults object with the presigned_urls list populated
```

classes.name: servicex.query_core.QueryStringGenerator

methods.name: servicex.query_core.QueryStringGenerator.generate_selection_string
documentation: override with the selection string to send to ServiceX
This abstract class just defines an interface to give the selection string

classes.name: servicex.query_core.GenericQueryStringGenerator

classes.name: servicex.func_adl.func_adl_dataset.FuncADLQuery

methods.name: servicex.func_adl.func_adl_dataset.FuncADLQuery.set_tree
documentation:
Set the tree name for the query.
Args:
    tree_name (str): Name of the tree to use for the query

Returns:
    The Dataset with the tree appended to the first call object

methods.name: servicex.func_adl.func_adl_dataset.FuncADLQuery.generate_qastle
documentation:
Generate the qastle from the ast of the query.

1. The top level function is already marked as being "ok"
1. If the top level function is something we have to process locally,
   then we strip it off.

Args:
    a (ast.AST): The complete AST of the request.

Returns:
    str: Qastle that should be sent to servicex

methods.name: servicex.func_adl.func_adl_dataset.FuncADLQuery.as_qastle
documentation:
Generate Qastle from this AST

:returns:
    Qastle representation of the target's AST
ServiceX Dataset class that uses func_adl query syntax.

classes.name: servicex.servicex_client.ReturnValueException
documentation: An exception occurred at some point while obtaining this result from ServiceX

classes.name: servicex.servicex_client.ServiceXClient

methods.name: servicex.servicex_client.ServiceXClient.__init__
documentation:
If both `backend` and `url` are unspecified then it will attempt to pick up
the default backend from `.servicex`

```
param backend

Name of a deployment from the .servicex file

param url

Direct URL of a serviceX deployment instead of using .servicex.
Can only work with hosts without auth, or the token is found
in a file pointed to by the environment variable BEARER_TOKEN_FILE

param config_path

Optional path te the  file. If not specified,
will search in local directory and up in enclosing directories
```

methods.name: servicex.servicex_client.ServiceXClient.get_transforms_async
documentation:
Retrieve all transforms you have run on the server
\:return: List of Transform status objects

methods.name: servicex.servicex_client.ServiceXClient.get_transform_status_async
documentation:
Get the status of a given transform
\:param transform_id: The uuid of the transform
\:return: The current status for the transform

methods.name: servicex.servicex_client.ServiceXClient.get_datasets
documentation:
Retrieve all datasets you have run on the server
\:return: List of Query objects

methods.name: servicex.servicex_client.ServiceXClient.get_dataset
documentation:
Retrieve a dataset by its ID
\:return: A Query object

methods.name: servicex.servicex_client.ServiceXClient.delete_dataset
documentation:
Delete a dataset by its ID
\:return: A Query object

methods.name: servicex.servicex_client.ServiceXClient.get_code_generators
documentation:
Retrieve the code generators deployed with the serviceX instance
:return:  The list of code generators as json dictionary

methods.name: servicex.servicex_client.ServiceXClient.generic_query
documentation:
Generate a Query object for a generic codegen specification

```
param dataset_identifier

The dataset identifier or filelist to be the source of files

param title

Title to be applied to the transform. This is also useful for
relating transform results.

param codegen

Name of the code generator to use with this transform

param result_format

Do you want Paqrquet or Root? This can be set later with
the set_result_format method

param ignore_cache

Ignore the query cache and always run the query

return

A Query object
```

Connection to a ServiceX deployment. Instances of this class can deployment
data from the service and also interact with previously run transformations.
Instances of this class are factories for `Datasets``

classes.name: servicex.databinder_models.Sample

methods.name: servicex.databinder_models.Sample.dataset_identifier
documentation: Access the dataset identifier for the sample.

methods.name: servicex.databinder_models.Sample.validate_did_xor_file
documentation:
Ensure that only one of Dataset, RootFile, or RucioDID is specified.
\:param values:
\:return:

methods.name: servicex.databinder_models.Sample.validate_nfiles_is_not_zero
documentation: Ensure that NFiles is not set to zero

methods.name: servicex.databinder_models.Sample.truncate_long_sample_name
documentation:
Truncate sample name to 128 characters if exceed
Print warning message

class_variables.name: servicex.databinder_models.Sample.Name
documentation:
The name of the sample. This makes it easier to identify the sample
in the output.

class_variables.name: servicex.databinder_models.Sample.Dataset
documentation: Dataset identifier for the sample

class_variables.name: servicex.databinder_models.Sample.NFiles
documentation:
Limit the Number of files to be used in the sample.
The DID Finder will guarantee the same files will be returned
between each invocation. Set to `None` to use all files.

class_variables.name: servicex.databinder_models.Sample.Query
documentation: Query string or query generator for the sample.

class_variables.name: servicex.databinder_models.Sample.IgnoreLocalCache
documentation: Flag to ignore local cache.

class_variables.name: servicex.databinder_models.Sample.Codegen
documentation:
Code generator name, if applicable. Generally users don't need to specify this. It is
implied by the query class

class_variables.name: servicex.databinder_models.Sample.RucioDID
documentation:
Rucio Dataset Identifier, if applicable.
    Deprecated: Use 'Dataset' instead.

class_variables.name: servicex.databinder_models.Sample.XRootDFiles
documentation:
XRootD file(s) associated with the sample.
        Deprecated: Use 'Dataset' instead.
Represents a single transform request within a larger submission.

classes.name: servicex.databinder_models.General

class_variables.name: servicex.databinder_models.General.Codegen
documentation:
Code generator name to be applied across all of the samples, if applicable.
Generally users don't need to specify this. It is implied by the query class

class_variables.name: servicex.databinder_models.General.OutputFormat
documentation: Output format for the transform request.

class_variables.name: servicex.databinder_models.General.Delivery
documentation: Specifies the delivery method for the output files.

class_variables.name: servicex.databinder_models.General.OutputDirectory
documentation: Directory to output a yaml file describing the output files.

class_variables.name: servicex.databinder_models.General.OutFilesetName
documentation: Name of the yaml file that will be created in the output directory.

inner_classes.name: servicex.databinder_models.General.OutputFormatEnum

methods.name: servicex.databinder_models.General.OutputFormatEnum.to_ResultFormat
documentation:
This method is used to convert the OutputFormatEnum enum to the ResultFormat enum,
which is what is actually used for the TransformRequest. This allows us to use
different string values in the two enum classes to maintain backend compatibility

class_variables.name: servicex.databinder_models.General.OutputFormatEnum.parquet
documentation:
Save the output as
a parquet file https://parquet.apache.org/

class_variables.name: servicex.databinder_models.General.OutputFormatEnum.root_ttree
documentation:
Save the output as
a ROOT TTree https://root.cern.ch/doc/master/classTTree.html
Specifies the output format for the transform request.

class_variables.name: servicex.databinder_models.General.DeliveryEnum.LocalCache
documentation:
Download the files to the local computer and store them in the cache.
Transform requests will return paths to these files in the cache

class_variables.name: servicex.databinder_models.General.DeliveryEnum.URLs
documentation: Return URLs to the files stored in the ServiceX object store
Represents a group of samples to be transformed together.

classes.name: servicex.databinder_models.ServiceXSpec

class_variables.name: servicex.databinder_models.ServiceXSpec.General
documentation: General settings for the transform request

class_variables.name: servicex.databinder_models.ServiceXSpec.Sample
documentation: List of samples to be transformed

class_variables.name: servicex.databinder_models.ServiceXSpec.Definition
documentation: Any reusable definitions that are needed for the transform request
ServiceX Submission Specification - pass this into the ServiceX `deliver` function

A group of datasets that are to be transformed together. This is a convenience
class to allow you to submit multiple datasets to a ServiceX instance and
then wait for all of them to complete.
